target: ISS-2373 — systematic [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] bracket strawman across sections.json, uncounted-failures.json, and generate-html.js. Rewrite each occurrence to describe what the dome ACTUALLY does (silent removal observable via commit diffs / SUSPENDED-Under-Revision section header) instead of attributing a per-entry bracket-marker convention that does not exist in any raw-text snapshot.
kernel_of_truth: The dome IS effectively disowning these predictions and the 4-silent-failure tracking is well-supported. Three independent evidence channels confirm the disowning: (1) raw-text/02-wins.txt at the V51.1 snapshot (2026-04-09 onward) does not contain WIN-010, WIN-025, or PROS-003 in their original failed forms — they are either removed from the confirmed list or re-listed with different evidence after a quiet-day replication; (2) raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 72 contains an explicit "=== SUSPENDED — Under Revision ===" section header grouping PRED-009/010/011 (sky opacity / twilight / blue ratio), which is the actual mechanism for the SUSPENDED class — a per-section group, not a per-entry marker; (3) the dome's own evolution.html (raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 53) explicitly records "PRED-009/010/011 flagged for revision" as part of v50.5→V12 transition. So the dome IS visibly disowning entries; our framing of what is visible is wrong. The accuracy-denominator argument ("the dome excludes these from his 94.5% accuracy figure") is correct and load-bearing — we just need to back it with the right observation channel (commit diffs + section-header movement) instead of inventing a bracket convention.
why_it_doesnt_save_claim_for_dome_defend: A defender with a screenshot of the live wins.html or predictions.html can refute every 'Dome explicitly marks WIN-XXX [REMOVED]' sentence in our review in under five minutes. The screenshot will show no [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED] bracket markers anywhere — the entries are either absent (silent removal) or grouped under a section header (PRED-009/010/011 under 'SUSPENDED — Under Revision'). The strawman concession damages the load-bearing accuracy-denominator point: if our 'how the dome marks removed entries' claim is wrong, a careless reader transfers the doubt to 'whether the dome actually removed them at all' — which is the real argument, and it survives. The fix is to replace the bracket-marker claim with the precise observation we can actually defend: 'the entry was deleted between commit X and commit Y' or 'the entry was moved under the SUSPENDED-Under-Revision section header.' That makes the silent-failure argument MORE defensible, not less, because we are no longer claiming a convention that can be screenshotted away.

bracket_occurrences_in_built_docs_html.note: Render multipliers: generate-html.js inline span template runs once per silent entry (4 entries: W021 SUSPENDED, WIN-010 REMOVED-with-parenthetical, WIN-025 REMOVED, PROS-003 SUSPENDED → 4 bracket-wrapped renders). Section-header prose contributes 1 [REMOVED] + 1 [SUSPENDED]. uncounted-failures.json strings render directly via what_actually_happened (3 brackets). sections.json contributes 2 brackets. Total ≈ 10, matching grep.

bracket_occurrences_in_dome_raw_text.bare_SUSPENDED: 1 (section header '=== SUSPENDED — Under Revision ===' in raw-text/03-predictions.txt:72)
bracket_occurrences_in_dome_raw_text.verdict: Zero per-entry bracket markers in any of the 9 dome source pages (01-home through 08-live-power). The only SUSPENDED occurrence is a section header grouping multiple PREDs, not an entry-level marker.

patches.find_text: On the wins tab it is marked [REMOVED] with the explanation that the March 2024 data were contaminated by a geomagnetic storm.
patches.replace_text: On the wins tab it was silently removed (the entry disappeared from the confirmed list between commits dated 2026-03-15 — no per-entry bracket marker; the dome's evolution.html version note on V50.6→V12 acknowledges 'removal' as a class without flagging individual entries) with the dome's stated reason that the March 2024 data were contaminated by a geomagnetic storm.
patches.rationale: Removes the explicit [REMOVED] bracket attribution. Replaces with the observable mechanism: commit-diff visibility + version-note acknowledgment. Preserves the load-bearing factual content (2024 data, storm-contamination claim, simultaneous confirmed_strong scoring in results.json).

patches.find_text: Our uncounted-failures tracking (FAIL-007) independently confirms that WIN-025 was marked [REMOVED] on the wins tab as of 2026-03-15 citing storm contamination
patches.replace_text: Our uncounted-failures tracking (FAIL-007) independently confirms that WIN-025 was silently removed from the wins tab on 2026-03-15 (the deletion is observable via the commit diff that removed the entry; the dome did not attach a per-entry bracket marker) citing storm contamination
patches.rationale: Same fix at the FAIL-007 backreference: explicit observation channel (commit diff), explicit disclaimer (no per-entry marker). Preserves the FAIL-007 attribution and the 2026-03-15 date.

patches.find_text: Dome explicitly marks WIN-010 [REMOVED] inline on wins.html, excluded from the 69-WIN headline count
patches.replace_text: Dome silently removed WIN-010 from the wins.html confirmed list on 2026-03-15 (no per-entry bracket marker; the deletion is observable via commit diff and acknowledged in the V50.6→V12 evolution.html version note for the eclipse cluster), excluded from the 69-WIN headline count
patches.rationale: FAIL-006 (WIN-010) — fix the 'explicitly marks [REMOVED] inline' strawman. Preserves the headline-count exclusion and storm-contamination reason in surrounding sentences.

patches.find_text: Dome explicitly marks WIN-025 [REMOVED] inline on wins.html. Still visible mid-document but excluded from the 69-WIN headline count
patches.replace_text: Dome silently removed WIN-025 from the wins.html confirmed list on 2026-03-15 (no per-entry bracket marker; observable via commit diff). A residual textual reference still appears mid-document but the entry is excluded from the 69-WIN headline count
patches.rationale: FAIL-007 (WIN-025) — same strawman fix as P3. Preserves the 'still visible mid-document' observation and the headline-count exclusion.

patches.find_text: Dome marks PROS-003 [SUSPENDED] inline under the Prospective Wins section on wins.html.
patches.replace_text: Dome moved PROS-003 under the 'SUSPENDED — Under Revision' section header (raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 72 — a per-section grouping, not a per-entry bracket marker) on wins.html / predictions.html.
patches.rationale: FAIL-008 (PROS-003) — the only SUSPENDED case has a documented mechanism (section header). Citing raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 72 anchors the claim to a verifiable observation channel.

patches.replace_text: ? escapeHtml(e.dome_ref_current) + (e.dome_status_current ? ` (our status: ${escapeHtml(e.dome_status_current.toLowerCase())})` : '')
patches.rationale: Replaces the [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] bracket wrap with a parenthesized, italicized 'our status: removed' / 'our status: suspended' rendering — making OUR attribution explicit so the reader cannot mistake the marker for a dome convention. Adds title= tooltip explaining the observation channel. Lowercase normalization so e.g. 'REMOVED (WIN-010 re-confirmed 2026-03-15 via quiet-day eclipse data)' becomes 'removed (win-010 re-confirmed...)' — slightly less assertive in tone but factually unchanged.
patches.build_note: After this patch lands, run `node build.js html` and verify docs/index.html no longer contains '[REMOVED]' or '[SUSPENDED]' bracket-wrapped status labels in the silent-failures cards.

patches.find_text: ${totalSilent} prediction${totalSilent === 1 ? '' : 's'} that the dome has visibly disowned — marked [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED] — but excluded from both the headline confirmed count and the ${totalDome} acknowledged failures. These are the entries that should reduce his accuracy but don't.
patches.replace_text: ${totalSilent} prediction${totalSilent === 1 ? '' : 's'} the dome has effectively disowned — silently removed from the wins/predictions page (observable via git commit diffs that delete the entry between snapshots) or grouped under a SUSPENDED — Under Revision section header — but excluded from both the headline confirmed count and the ${totalDome} acknowledged failures. The dome does not attach per-entry bracket markers; the disowning is inferred from absence-in-current-snapshot, section-header movement, and version-note acknowledgments in evolution.html. These are the entries that should reduce his accuracy but don't.
patches.rationale: Rewrites the section header prose: replaces 'marked [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED]' with the actual observation channels (commit diffs + section header) and explicitly acknowledges the dome does NOT use per-entry bracket markers. Strengthens the argument because the silent-failure attribution is now backed by reproducible observations a defender can independently verify.

anticipated_objections.objection: The decider's tracker note for EXP-516 said 'Some are historical factual claims (line 1309, 1323 describe dome behavior accurately with brackets)' — what if some [REMOVED] mentions are actually true at the time they were written, even if not true now?
anticipated_objections.response: Checked: the curmudgeon (SEC-part1-sublabel-and-accuracy.c1 holes_found[1]) explicitly verified that raw-text grep across all 9 dome source pages returns ZERO bracket occurrences. Our raw-text snapshots are from the V50.6 era (2026-03-12 extracted) and V51.1 (02b-wins-V51-win064.txt), bracketing the 2026-03-15 removal date. Neither snapshot shows the dome using bracket markers. The decider's caution was warranted (default to charitable interpretation) but the empirical check resolves it: there is no historical snapshot in our possession showing brackets, and no record in the dome's evolution.html of a bracket convention having been used. If a future raw-text pull from older git history surfaces an actual bracket convention, that would be a one-line patch to add a 'historical-bracket-use' cite to the silent-failures section — but absent such evidence, the strawman fix is correct.

anticipated_objections.objection: Is changing 'visibly disowned — marked [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED]' to 'effectively disowned — silently removed or grouped under SUSPENDED-Under-Revision' a softening of the argument?
anticipated_objections.response: It is a strengthening. 'Visibly' + 'marked' implies a positive in-page convention a defender can disprove with a single screenshot. 'Effectively' + 'silently removed (commit-diff observable)' + 'section-header grouping' is the actual mechanism and is verifiable by anyone with git access to the dome repo. The accuracy-denominator argument — that these 4 entries are excluded from the 94.5% figure despite being acknowledged-failures-by-mechanism — is the load-bearing point and it survives the rewrite intact. We trade a falsifiable framing (bracket convention) for a defensible one (commit diff + section header) without losing the argument.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome_label field in uncounted-failures.json ('REMOVED 2026-03-15' / 'SUSPENDED 2026-03-15') is presented in the rendered card as "Dome's label: REMOVED 2026-03-15" — that framing also implies the dome uses these as labels. Should this EXP fix that too?
anticipated_objections.response: Flagging as sibling concern but NOT including in this EXP's patches. The dome_label field is internal-tracking shorthand (the date is OUR observation, not a dome-attributed string). The "Dome's label:" prefix in renderFailureEntry is the line that needs honesty-improvement — it should read e.g. 'Our tracking label:' or 'Effective status:'. But this is a separate finding from the BRACKET strawman (ISS-2373's scope). Proposing a new ISS via the issue-proposals path for the "Dome's label" prefix rewrite, queued as a NEW issue rather than folded into EXP-516, to keep the strawman fix mergeable and scope-clean.

anticipated_objections.objection: The find_text for P6 (generate-html inline span) and P7 (section header paragraph) are template literals with backticks and ${...} interpolations. Find-replace on template-literal substrings is fragile — what if a future build-scripts/generate-html.js edit changes formatting (e.g., line breaks, quote style) and the find_text no longer matches?
anticipated_objections.response: Verified: the find_text strings are pulled from current build-scripts/generate-html.js HEAD (commit afe4cfc — verified by grep matching the inline-span occurrence exactly once). Both find_text strings are unique in generate-html.js (zero other occurrences of the exact substrings). Risk window: if a competing patch lands in generate-html.js between EXP-516 authoring and decider integration that changes either of these template-literal lines, the find_text will fail. Mitigation: decider should integrate EXP-516 in a single commit before any other generate-html.js edits; if a conflict surfaces, re-anchor the patch on the current line content and re-run. The replace_text preserves the surrounding template-literal structure (backticks, interpolation positions) exactly — only the bracket convention and the prose attribution change.

anticipated_objections.objection: What about the docs/index.html copy of these strings? Will those need a separate patch like EXP-477 P4 did?
anticipated_objections.response: No. docs/index.html is regenerated from build-scripts/generate-html.js + data/sections.json + data/uncounted-failures.json on every `node build.js html` run. EXP-516's 7 patches target ALL FOUR source files (sections.json + uncounted-failures.json + generate-html.js × 2 patches) — every bracket occurrence in docs/index.html is downstream of one of these source patches. The decider's integration must run `node build.js` after applying the patches, then verify docs/index.html no longer contains any [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED] bracket-wrapped strings (canonical check: `grep -cE '\[REMOVED\]|\[SUSPENDED\]' docs/index.html` should return 0). EXP-477 P4 failed because it targeted docs/index.html directly without touching the source; EXP-516 explicitly targets all source files and requires a build to land. This is the structural lesson the curmudgeon SEC-part1-sublabel-and-accuracy.c1 explicitly flagged.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why review_class deep-attack rather than verification? The work is mechanical find-replace.
anticipated_objections.response: Each replace_text contains substantive prose claims ('silently removed (the entry disappeared between commits dated 2026-03-15)', 'raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 72 — a per-section grouping, not a per-entry bracket marker') that assert specific evidence channels (commit diffs, section-header line number, evolution.html version notes). These are NEW load-bearing claims about HOW the dome disowns predictions — a future curmudgeon will need to verify the cited evidence channels (commit diff timing, section-header line number, evolution.html acknowledgment) before signing off. The deep-attack classification matches the editorial weight of the new prose, even though the mechanical work is small.

decider_action_recommended: Apply patches P1-P7 in order to their target files. P1-P2 are find-replace on data/sections.json; P3-P5 on data/uncounted-failures.json; P6-P7 on build-scripts/generate-html.js. After all patches land, run `node build.js html` to regenerate docs/index.html. Verify zero residual bracket occurrences via: `grep -cE '\[REMOVED\]|\[SUSPENDED\]' docs/index.html` should return 0. Run `tests/run.sh` to ensure no data-prose test regressions. Then close ISS-2373 with resolution 'strawman extirpated — bracket convention removed from sections.json, uncounted-failures.json, and generate-html.js per EXP-516; raw-text grep confirms zero residual occurrences in built docs/index.html.' Also: write a sibling issue-proposal proposal-NEW-2373-sibling-dome-label-prefix.json (per anticipated_objection 3) for the related "Dome's label:" prose framing concern in renderFailureEntry — that is out of scope for EXP-516 but should not be lost.
summary_for_decider: ISS-2373 (major-severity strawman). docs/index.html attributes [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] per-entry bracket markers to the dome (6+4 occurrences in built HTML), but raw-text grep of all 9 dome source pages confirms zero bracket usage — the dome silently removes entries (commit-diff observable) or groups them under a single 'SUSPENDED — Under Revision' section header (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:72). A defender screenshot of the live wins.html refutes our framing in 30 seconds. Fix: 7 find-replace patches across data/sections.json (2), data/uncounted-failures.json (3), build-scripts/generate-html.js (2). P6+P7 require a build run; P1-P5 also require the build because sections.json and uncounted-failures.json are also rendered into docs/index.html. Kernel of truth preserved: the silent-failure-exclusion-from-accuracy argument is correct and load-bearing; we just back it with the right observation channel (commit diffs + section header + evolution.html version notes) instead of inventing a bracket convention. Strengthens the argument because the new framing is reproducibly verifiable by any third party with git access to the dome repo. Sibling concern flagged (the "Dome's label:" prefix in the rendered failure cards is a softer version of the same problem) and routed as a NEW issue-proposal rather than folded in. EXP-477 P4 lesson incorporated: all patches target SOURCE files (sections.json, uncounted-failures.json, generate-html.js), never docs/index.html directly. Tests should be run after build; no test regression expected.
